chapter.title: Title page and table of contents
chapter.note: Added leaf with title and table of contents written in a thirteenth- or fourteenth-century hand; additional note below others added in the fifteenth or sixteenth century: "Item Acta sancti Augustini contra Fortunatum in modu dialogi"

chapter.title: Illuminated title page
chapter.note: Decorative title page added in late nineteenth or early twentieth century at time of rebinding

chapter.title: In Iohannis epistulam ad Parthos 1.12 (excerpt)
chapter.incipit: Qui diligit fratrem suum in lumine manet
chapter.note: Excerpt of text, on lines 1-13, ending with "...in unitate caritatis est fraterna dilectio."
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: De genesi ad litteram, X, v, 8 (excerpt)
chapter.note: Excerpt of text, on lines 14-16, ending with "...flatus ipse processit."
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: Contra adversarium legis et prophetarum libri duo, I, xiv, 18 (excerpt)
chapter.incipit: Dictum est cum laude de domino
chapter.note: Excerpt of text, on lines 16-20, ending with "...non nouerat per experientiam."
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: Sermo CLVI (excerpts)
chapter.note: Two different excerpts from same text; Sermo CLVI, xii, 13: lines 20 (fol. 24v)-1 (fol. 25r), ending with "...sed miserentis dei est"; Sermo CLVI, xiv, 15 through xv, 16: lines 1-5 (fol. 25r), beginning with "Sed accepistis spiritum..." and ending with "...spiritui nostro quia sumus filii dei."
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: Liber de spiritu sancto interprete Hieronymo (excerpts)
chapter.incipit: Nam eundem evangelii locum Matheus Lucas describens
chapter.note: Many small excerpts beginning on fol. 25r, line 5, and ending on fol. 25v, line 27; excerpts are, as found in Migne, Patrologia Græca 39: 1035 D 3 - 1036 A 8; 1037 A 1-4, B 7-13, C 2-7, 10-12; 1040 A 6-15; 1060 B 7-13; 1060 B 15 - 1061 B 2; 1062 A 15 - B 5; 1063 A 7-8; 1066 A 9-10; 1082 C 10-15; 1083 A 1-D 1
chapter.author: Didymos, Alexandreus

chapter.title: De Genesi contra Manichaeos
chapter.rubric: Primus liber exameron sancti augustini in genesi incipit.
chapter.note: Full text of De Genesi; fols. 26r-39r: Book I; fols. 39r-53v: Book II
Explicit on fol. 53v appears to be scrambled, and small letters above each word seem to indicate correct word order
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: Anonymous text I
chapter.rubric: Responsiones contra hereticorum inpugnationes quas contra libros augustini de praedestinatis opposverunt incipiunt.

chapter.title: Anonymous text II
chapter.rubric: Faciamus hominem ad imaginem et similitudinem nostram.
chapter.note: Text is divided into thirty-three smaller parts, each with a title

chapter.title: Distych referring to Charles the Bald
chapter.note: The distych is found on lines 23-24, and reads: PERLEGE REX SAPIENS. SOPHIAE PIA DOGMATA SANCTAE/SICQUE FIDEM FIRMA PECTORE ET ORE TUO.
A note in the Walters file by Bernard Bischoff suggests this is a reference to Charles the Bald (see also Jeauneau, 1982, in bibliography)

chapter.title: Adversus quinque haereses
chapter.incipit: Debitor sum fratres fateor non necessitate
chapter.author: Quodvultdeus, Bishop of Carthage

chapter.title: De duabus animabus
chapter.rubric: Incipit liber aurelii augustini de duabus animabus.
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: Contra Fortunatum
chapter.rubric: Incipit Augustini presbyteri catholici [contra Fortunatum (an early addition)]
chapter.author: St. Augustine

chapter.title: Psalm 18.2
chapter.note: The same text was added in the tenth or eleventh century, three times by three hands: "celi enarrant gloriam dei"; two versions include musical notation
